task_completion.description: Open the properties or edit dialog for the first waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties, attribute table, or edit dialog for the first waypoint in the Fells Loop dataset is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the specific descriptive text for the first waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The comment or description field for the first waypoint contains exactly: 'Field station alpha - Trail junction, blazed intersection with spur trail. GPS signal strong. Soil sample FS-001 collected.'

task_completion.description: Open the properties or edit dialog for the second waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties, attribute table, or edit dialog for the second waypoint in the Fells Loop dataset is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the specific descriptive text for the second waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The comment or description field for the second waypoint contains exactly: 'Field station beta - Rocky outcrop overlook with exposed bedrock. Canopy gap provides clear satellite reception. Photo reference IMG_2847.'

task_completion.description: Initiate the export process for the dataset as a GPX file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As', 'Export', or 'Save Vector Layer' dialog is open with the output format explicitly set to GPX.

task_completion.description: Set the export file path to the required location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file name or path in the export dialog is set to 'C:\workspace\output\annotated_fells.gpx' before saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIS/mapping application's GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The edits and exports are performed within a graphical GIS or mapping application (e.g., QGIS, Google Earth), not by manually editing the file in a text editor or IDE.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing python scripts, sed/awk commands, or CLI tools being used to inject the text or generate the GPX file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the text was entered into the correct application fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The descriptive text is clearly visible inside the application's native waypoint description/comment input fields, rather than pasted into a generic notepad or terminal.
